title: Rare beef phở and undercooked foods: pregnancy safety
excerpt: General food-safety rules for Vietnamese favorites — phở tái (rare beef), salads, and storage at home.
content:
## General principles

Heat kills many pathogens. In pregnancy, prefer **well-done** meat and seafood.

## Common Vietnamese dishes

- **Phở tái (rare beef slices):** ask for well-done beef (tái chín kỹ) or choose chicken phở with fully cooked meat.
- **Gỏi / raw salads:** ensure vegetables are washed; prefer cooked salads if hygiene is uncertain.
- **Blood pudding, raw eggs:** avoid.

## Storage

Refrigerate leftovers within 2 hours; reheat thoroughly. Do not eat room-temperature broth with meat left out long hours.
metaTitle: Phở and undercooked food safety in pregnancy | Bầu Ăn Gì? Blog
metaDescription: How to enjoy Vietnamese dishes safely in pregnancy: cook meat thoroughly, handle leftovers properly.
